news.headline: Why XLK Investors Pay Twice for the Same Mega-Cap Exposure
news.summary: XLK's expense ratio looks almost free, but the fee that will actually hurt your portfolio never appears on the factsheet. Before you add another tech position, check what you are quietly paying twice for.

keyLevels.note: Leading the tape at $696.06 — key for risk appetite
Pivot $742.09 — bulls want to hold above

gamePlan: Bias into the open: Mixed — watch only — wait for confirmation.
Signals are conflicted — let the first 30 minutes set the tone before committing risk.
Use QQQ $696.06 as the risk-on/off line; trade with it, not against it.
Primary idea: TSLA PUT — enter $15.42–$17.04, target $24.34+, stop $9.74.
Keep total options exposure modest; one A+ setup beats five marginal ones.
report.narrative: Futures point to a softer open with QQQ +0.11% and SPY -0.16%. Composite sentiment -1 (Mixed). The tape is being driven mainly by vix, sector rotation, market breadth (a/d). VIX at 14.03 (-1.94%). Environment read: watch only — wait for confirmation. Technology (+0.95%) is leading while Healthcare (-2.73%) lags. Premarket strength in MSFT, COIN; weakness in TSLA, LLY. The desk's highest-conviction setup is TSLA (6.5/10).
report.riskWarning: Options trading is risky and can result in the loss of the entire premium paid. This report provides analysis and educational information only — not financial advice. Size positions small (1–2% of capital per idea), honor stops, and do your own research.
